Multi-physics package¶

src.multi_physics.solver¶

Coupled multi-physics solver for electrostatic actuation of a cantilever beam (air electrostatics coupled to modal mechanics), implemented in FEniCSx with remeshing at each time step.

  • Mesh template: it uses MICRONS as the unit, then mesh coordinates are converted to meters in the code for SI consistency.

  • Electrostatic force: given number of mode shapes, Maxwell traction is projected onto the first cantilever mode shapes (transverse-only) on tag=10 (force_segment).

  • Mechanics (diagonal modal system):

    \[m_i \ddot{q}_i + c_i \dot{q}_i + k_i q_i = F_i, \quad i = 1 \ldots 4,\]

    where \(k_i = m_i \omega_i^2\) and \(c_i = 2 \zeta_i \sqrt{m_i k_i}\), integrated with Newmark average acceleration \((\beta = 1/4,\ \gamma = 1/2)\).

Example usage:

python -m coupled_modal_electro --template-geo geometry.geo --dt 1e-5 --nsteps 200 --Vdc 0 --Vac 5 --freq 2.5e3 --Vupper 0 --Vouter 0 --omega 6.3e5 3.9e6 1.1e7 2.1e7 --mass 1e-12 1e-12 1e-12 1e-12 --zeta 0.01 0.01 0.01 0.01 --print-every 1 --fail-fast

There are several optional arguments to customize the behavior:

  • --template-geo: Path to the Gmsh geometry template file (required). This file should contain placeholders __COEFF1__, __COEFF2__, __COEFF3__, and __COEFF4__ which will be replaced by the current modal coefficients (in microns) at each time step.

  • --workdir: Base directory for output files (default: “coupled_work”). Meshes will be saved in workdir/meshes and results in workdir/results.

  • --gmsh: Path to the Gmsh executable (default: “gmsh”).

  • --mshver: Gmsh mesh format version to use (“2.2” or “4.1”, default: “4.1”). Note that the code currently expects physical tags to be integers, which is the case in both versions, but the internal handling of tags differs between versions. Version 4.1 is recommended for better performance and support for larger meshes.

  • --dt: Time step size for the Newmark integration (default: 1e-6 seconds).

  • --nsteps: Total number of time steps to simulate (default: 200).

  • --xmin-um, --L-um, --thickness-um: Geometric parameters of the cantilever beam in microns (default: -50, 100, 10).

  • --Vupper, --Vouter: Voltages for the upper conductor and outer boundary (default: 0.0 V). Use --no-outer-bc to apply natural Neumann conditions on the outer boundary instead of a Dirichlet condition.

  • --epsr: Relative permittivity of the medium (default: 1.0).

  • --Vdc, --Vac, --freq: Parameters for the time-varying voltage applied to the lower conductor: DC offset, AC amplitude, and frequency in Hz (default: 0 V, 20 V, 1e5 Hz).

  • --omega, --mass, --zeta: Mechanical parameters for the 4 modes: natural frequencies (rad/s), modal masses (kg), and damping ratios (default: 0.01 for all modes).

  • --print-every: Frequency of printing diagnostics to the console (default: every step).

  • --fail-fast: If set, the simulation will check for basic mesh quality and tag presence at each step and will abort if the mesh looks broken (e.g., too few nodes/cells or missing critical tags). This can help catch issues early in the remeshing process.

  • --min-nodes, --min-cells: Thresholds for the minimum number of nodes and cells in the mesh when --fail-fast is enabled (default: 2000 each).

src.multi_physics.solver.V_lower_time(t, Vdc, Vac, freq_hz)[source]¶

Description

Compute the lower voltage as a function of time.

Parameters:

  • t (float) – Time (s).

  • Vdc (float) – DC voltage (V).

  • Vac (float) – AC voltage amplitude (V).

  • freq_hz (float) – Frequency (Hz).

Return type:

float

Returns:

  • V_lower (float) – Lower voltage at time t (V).

src.multi_physics.solver.cantilever_shape(xi, beta, L)[source]¶

Description

Compute the cantilever mode shape function. The mode shape is given by

\[\psi_i(\xi) = \cosh(\beta_i \xi) - \cos(\beta_i \xi) - C_i \left( \sinh(\beta_i \xi) - \sin(\beta_i \xi) \right)\]

where

\[C_i = \frac{\cosh(\beta_i L) + \cos(\beta_i L)}{\sinh(\beta_i L) + \sin(\beta_i L)}.\]
Parameters:

  • xi (Expr) – The spatial coordinate along the beam, \(\xi = x - x_{\min}\).

  • beta (float) – The mode parameter \(\beta_i\) for the i-th mode, related to the natural frequency.

  • L (float) – The cantilever beam length.

Return type:

Expr

Returns:

  • psi (ufl.core.expr.Expr) – The computed mode shape function evaluated at xi.

src.multi_physics.solver.energy_and_cap(domain, phi, Vdiff, eps_r=1.0, eps0=8.8541878128e-12)[source]¶

Description

Compute the electrostatic energy W stored in the system and an effective capacitance C=2W/Vdiff^2 based on the potential distribution phi and the voltage difference Vdiff.

Parameters:

  • domain – The FEniCSx mesh domain object.

  • phi – The electrostatic potential function defined on the mesh.

  • Vdiff – The voltage difference between the conductors \((V_{\text{lower}} - V_{\text{upper}})\).

  • eps_r – Relative permittivity of the medium (default: 1.0).

  • eps0 – Vacuum permittivity (default: 8.8541878128e-12 F/m).

Return type:

tuple[float, float]

Returns:

  • W (float) – The computed electrostatic energy in joules.

  • C (float) – The computed effective capacitance in farads.

src.multi_physics.solver.main()[source]¶
src.multi_physics.solver.make_mesh_step(template_geo, workdir, step, coeff_m, gmsh_exec='gmsh', mshver='4.1')[source]¶

Description

Generate a Gmsh mesh for the given time step by rendering the geometry template with the current modal coefficients and running Gmsh to produce a mesh file.

Parameters:

  • template_geo (Path) – Path to the Gmsh geometry template file.

  • workdir (Path) – Directory where the generated mesh file will be saved.

  • step (int) – Current time step index (used for naming the output files).

  • coeff_m (ndarray) – Array of modal coefficients in meters (shape (4,)) to substitute into the geometry template.

  • gmsh_exec (str) – Name or path of the Gmsh executable to run (default: “gmsh”).

  • mshver (str) – Gmsh mesh format version to use (“2.2” or “4.1”, default: “4.1”).

Return type:

Path

Returns:

  • msh_path (Path) – Path to the generated Gmsh mesh file for this time step.

src.multi_physics.solver.mesh_stats(domain)[source]¶

Description

Compute basic statistics about the mesh, including the number of nodes, number of cells, and bounding box coordinates.

Parameters:

domain – The FEniCSx mesh domain object.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

  • stats (dict) – A dictionary containing mesh statistics: number of nodes, number of cells, and bounding box coordinates.

src.multi_physics.solver.modal_forces_4(domain, facet_tags, phi, betas, xmin_m, L_m, thickness_m, eps_r=1.0, eps0=8.8541878128e-12)[source]¶

Description

Compute the modal forces \(F_i\) for the first 4 modes by integrating the Maxwell traction over the force segment (tag 10) weighted by the mode shape functions. The force for each mode is given by:

\[F_i = \text{thickness} \cdot \int_{ds(10)} \mathbf{t}_{\text{beam}} \cdot \boldsymbol{\psi}_i \, ds, \quad i=1..4 \text{ with } \boldsymbol{\psi}_i = (0, \text{mode}_i(x)).\]

The Maxwell traction is computed from the electric field \(\mathbf{E} = -\nabla \phi\) and the permittivity, and the mode shapes are evaluated at the spatial coordinate \(\xi = x - x_{\min}\) along the beam.

Parameters:

  • domain – The FEniCSx mesh domain object.

  • facet_tags – The facet tags object from the FEniCSx mesh, which contains information about the physical tags assigned to facets.

  • phi – The electrostatic potential function defined on the mesh.

  • betas (ndarray) – Array of mode parameters (beta_i) for the first 4 modes, in units of 1/m.

  • xmin_m (float) – The minimum x-coordinate of the beam in meters (used to compute xi).

  • L_m (float) – The length of the cantilever beam in meters (used in the mode shape function).

  • thickness_m (float) – The thickness of the beam in meters (used to scale the integrated force).

  • eps_r (float) – Relative permittivity of the medium (default: 1.0).

  • eps0 (float) – Vacuum permittivity (default: 8.8541878128e-12 F/m).

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

  • F (np.ndarray) – Array of modal forces for the first 4 modes.

src.multi_physics.solver.newmark_step_diag(M, C, K, q, qd, qdd, F, dt, beta=0.25, gamma=0.5)[source]¶

Description

Perform one time step of the Newmark average acceleration method for a diagonal modal system, where M, C, K are arrays of modal masses, damping coefficients, and stiffnesses for each mode. The function updates the modal coordinates q, velocities qd, and accelerations qdd based on the applied forces F and the time step dt. In particular, we have

\[M qdd + C qd + K q = F \quad ext{for each mode},\]

and the Newmark update equations are applied in a vectorized manner for all modes simultaneously.

Parameters:

  • M – Array of modal masses for each mode.

  • C – Array of modal damping coefficients for each mode.

  • K – Array of modal stiffnesses for each mode.

  • q – Array of modal coordinates at the current time step.

  • qd – Array of modal velocities at the current time step.

  • qdd – Array of modal accelerations at the current time step.

  • F – Array of modal forces applied at the current time step.

  • dt – Time step size for the integration.

  • beta – Newmark beta parameter (default: 0.25 for average acceleration).

  • gamma – Newmark gamma parameter (default: 0.5 for average acceleration).

Returns:

  • q_new (np.ndarray) – Updated modal coordinates after the time step.

  • qd_new (np.ndarray) – Updated modal velocities after the time step.

  • qdd_new (np.ndarray) – Updated modal accelerations after the time step.

src.multi_physics.solver.project_E_dg0(domain, phi)[source]¶

Description

Project the electric field E = -grad(phi) onto a discontinuous Galerkin (DG0) function space to compute the cell-wise average electric field magnitude, which is used for diagnostics.

Parameters:

  • domain – The FEniCSx mesh domain object.

  • phi – The electrostatic potential function defined on the mesh.

Return type:

Function

Returns:

  • Eh (fem.Function) – A function in the DG0 space representing the projected electric field magnitude.

src.multi_physics.solver.render_geo_template(template_text, coeff_um)[source]¶

Description

Replace __COEFF1__, __COEFF2__, __COEFF3__, __COEFF4__ in the template text with the given coefficients (in microns).

Parameters:

  • template_text (str) – The Gmsh geometry template text.

  • coeff_um (ndarray) – Array of coefficients in microns.

Return type:

str

Returns:

  • geo_text (str) – The rendered Gmsh geometry text with coefficients substituted.

src.multi_physics.solver.run(cmd)[source]¶

Description

Run a subprocess command and raise an error if it fails, including the output in the error message.

Parameters:

cmd (list[str]) – List of command arguments to run (e.g., [“gmsh”, “-2”, “mesh.geo”, “-o”, “mesh.msh”]).

Return type:

None

src.multi_physics.solver.solve_electrostatics_one(msh_path, V_lower, V_upper=0.0, V_outer=None, eps_r=1.0)[source]¶

Description

Solve the electrostatic potential distribution for a given mesh and boundary conditions. The mesh is read from the specified Gmsh file, and Dirichlet boundary conditions are applied on the lower, upper, and optionally outer boundaries. The function returns the FEniCSx domain, the computed potential function, and the facet tags for further processing.

Parameters:

  • msh_path (Path) – Path to the Gmsh mesh file to read.

  • V_lower (float) – Voltage to apply on the lower conductor (tag 12).

  • V_upper (float) – Voltage to apply on the upper conductor (tags 10 and 11, default: 0.0 V).

  • V_outer (Optional[float]) – Voltage to apply on the outer boundary (tag 20). If None, natural Neumann conditions are applied instead (default: None).

  • eps_r (float) – Relative permittivity of the medium (default: 1.0).

Return type:

tuple

Returns:

  • domain (mesh.Mesh) – The FEniCSx domain object.

  • phi (fem.Function) – The computed electrostatic potential function.

  • facet_tags (mesh.MeshTags) – The facet tags for further processing.

src.multi_physics.solver.tag_counts(facet_tags)[source]¶

Description

Count the number of facets for each relevant tag (10, 11, 12, 20) in the facet_tags object.

Parameters:

facet_tags – The facet tags object from the FEniCSx mesh, which contains information about the physical tags assigned to facets.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

  • tags (dict) – A dictionary containing the count of facets for each tag of interest (10, 11, 12, 20).

src.multi_physics.solve_electrostatics_series¶

Solve electrostatics on a series of meshes (e.g. from a time-stepping .geo) and write XDMF for ParaView.

Example usage:

python -m multi_physics.solve_electrostatics_series --mesh-dir path/to/meshes --pattern "step_*.msh" --outdir electro_out --Vlower 1.0 --Vupper 0.0 --Vouter 0.0 --epsr 1.0
src.multi_physics.solve_electrostatics_series.main()[source]¶
src.multi_physics.solve_electrostatics_series.solve_one_mesh(msh_path, V_lower, V_upper, V_outer=0.0, eps_r=1.0)[source]¶

Solve electrostatics on one air-only mesh.

Description

Given a mesh with physical tags for the upper plate (tags 10+11), lower plate (tag 12), and optionally outer boundary (tag 20), solve the electrostatic potential distribution phi by applying Dirichlet boundary conditions on these tags. Then compute the electric field E = -grad(phi) and project it to a DG0 vector field for visualization. Finally, compute diagnostics such as the electrostatic energy stored in the system and an effective capacitance based on the potential distribution.

Parameters:

  • msh_path (Path) – Path to the input mesh file in .msh format.

  • V_lower (float) – Dirichlet potential to apply on the lower plate (tag 12).

  • V_upper (float) – Dirichlet potential to apply on the upper plate (tags 10+11).

  • V_outer (float | None) – Dirichlet potential to apply on the outer boundary (tag 20). If None, no Dirichlet condition is applied on the outer boundary (natural Neumann).

  • eps_r (float) – Relative permittivity of the medium (default: 1.0 for air).

Return type:

tuple[Function, Function, dict]

Returns:

  • phi_h (fem.Function) – The computed electrostatic potential distribution on the mesh.

  • E (fem.Function) – The computed electric field vector projected to a DG0 function space for visualization.

  • info (dict) – A dictionary containing diagnostics such as electrostatic energy and effective capacitance.
